papers.title_en: When Agents Lie: Premeditation, Persistence, and Exploitation in Repeated Games
papers.authors: Jerick Shi, Terry Jingcheng Zhang, Bernhard Schölkopf, Vincent Conitzer, Zhijing Jin
papers.evidence: 直接研究LLM智能体在重复博弈中的说谎行为，分析预谋性偏离
papers.tldr: 随着大语言模型智能体在行动前宣告意图，其承诺可信性成为关键安全问题。本研究通过三阶段协议（私下意图、公开宣告、最终行动）在六种重复博弈中测试三个前沿模型，发现背离宣告多为预谋（最高欺骗条件下超90%），但同一模型从完全诚实到几乎全部背离。不同模型对宣告的解释不相容，一些视为有约束力的承诺，另一些视为廉价交谈，导致收益差距自第0轮起持续至第10轮。因此，部署混合模型系统前必须对宣告语义进行实证测试，不应假定统一承诺。
papers.abstract_en: As large language models are deployed as autonomous agents that communicate intentions before acting, a critical safety question is whether agents that publicly commit to actions will honor those commitments. We place LLM agents in repeated $n$-player games with a three-stage protocol that separates private intent, public announcement, and final action, allowing us to identify whether each deviation from a stated announcement was already planned during private deliberation. Evaluating three frontier models across six games in homogeneous and heterogeneous groups over 10 rounds, we report two findings. First, when agents deviate from their announcements, the deviation is predominantly already stated in their private plan (exceeding 90% in the highest-deception conditions), yet this is not a fixed model property: the same model ranges from perfect honesty to near-total deviation across games. Second, different models interpret announcements incompatibly, some as binding commitments and others as cheap talk, producing payoff gaps that emerge in Round~0 and persist across all 10 rounds. Systems that combine models from different providers therefore cannot assume shared announcement semantics and require empirical testing of model interactions before deployment.

papers.evidence: 对 dueling Q-learning 的理论分析，游戏AI的核心强化学习技术
papers.tldr: Q-learning是强化学习基础算法，dueling架构通过分解Q函数为价值与优势函数提高效率，但纯表格更新的收敛性理论不足。本文给出中心化表格分解的直接解释，为未正则化常数步长递归建立收敛保证，推导确定性和随机版本的误差界。分析揭示价值和优势更新作为不同增益作用于Q函数的动作公共与动作差异成分，完善了dueling Q-learning的理论基础，为算法设计提供理论指导。
papers.abstract_en: Q-learning is a fundamental algorithm in reinforcement learning (RL) for solving discounted Markov decision processes (MDPs) when the transition kernel is unknown. The deep Q-network (DQN) extends Q-learning by using a deep neural network for Q-function approximation, which makes Q-learning applicable to more practical high-dimensional problems. Dueling Q-learning decomposes the Q-function into a value function and an advantage function and learns the two components jointly, which can improve learning efficiency. However, the theoretical understanding of dueling Q-learning is still limited. Recent work has initiated an analysis of tabular dueling Q-learning, but existing guarantees focus on a regularized formulation and leave the pure tabular update less completely understood. This paper strengthens that line of analysis by adding a direct interpretation of the centered tabular decomposition and by establishing convergence guarantees for the unregularized, unprojected constant step-size recursion. In particular, we derive an exact switching linear system representation for deterministic dueling Q-learning and a finite-time error bound in expectation for the sampled stochastic version. The analysis clarifies how the value and advantage updates act as different gains on the action-common (value function) and action-differential (advantage function) components of the Q-function.

papers.title_en: Playing ZendoWorld: Challenging AI Agents on Active Visual Concept Induction
papers.authors: Sophia Koehler, Antonia Wüst, Inga Ibs, Wasu Top Piriyakulkij, Wolfgang Stammer, Constantin Rothkopf, Kevin Ellis, Kristian Kersting
papers.evidence: 引入一个交互式游戏环境，让AI智能体推断视觉概念，评估游戏类智能体
papers.tldr: 构建能从视觉观察中主动归纳隐藏概念的智能体是AI领域的核心挑战，现有模型难以通过设计有信息实验来测试假设。ZendoWorld为此提出一个交互式视觉游戏环境，要求智能体推断逻辑规则，并通过提出新场景获取信息。实验评估了VLM、贝叶斯粒子滤波、动态概念发现和神经符号四类方法，发现高标签预测准确率并不意味着恢复了底层规则，感知与归纳分别成为不同智能体类别的瓶颈；基于VLM的智能体提出的实验近乎无信息，未能降低假设不确定性。与人类表现对比进一步揭示AI在归纳推理上的显著差距，尤其对复杂规则。该平台为科学发现等领域的智能体评估与改进提供了关键基准和前进方向。
papers.abstract_en: A central challenge in building intelligent systems is enabling agents to jointly perceive complex inputs, form hypotheses about hidden patterns, and design informative experiments to test them. To study this problem, we propose ZendoWorld, a controlled interactive environment in which agents must infer a logical rule about visual game observations, acquire information by proposing new scenes, and refine their hypotheses based on feedback from the game environment. We evaluate several agents spanning pure VLM reasoning, Bayesian particle filtering, dynamic concept discovery, and neuro-symbolic methods. Our main findings are: (1) high accuracy in predicting labels for observed examples does not imply recovery of the underlying rule; (2) perception and induction are distinct bottlenecks for different agent classes; and (3) VLM-based agents propose near-uninformative experiments, failing to actively reduce hypothesis uncertainty. To compare these results, we collect human data on the task, which reveals a gap in inductive reasoning, particularly for more complex rules. Overall, ZENDOWORLD takes an important step toward evaluating intelligent agents and identifies concrete avenues for improvement, particularly in domains like scientific discovery.

papers.title_en: Gimitest: A Comprehensive Tool for Testing Reinforcement Learning Policies
papers.authors: Dennis Gross, Quentin Mazouni, Helge Spieker, Arnaud Gotlieb
papers.evidence: 支持Gym和PettingZoo的RL策略测试工具，可用于游戏AI bot开发
papers.tldr: 强化学习策略的安全性与可靠性测试是当前痛点，现有自动化方法局限于特定环境和算法。为突破此限制，本文提出统一测试框架Gimitest，支持单/多智能体策略，兼容多种gym环境并允许灵活修改组件。在Farama Gymnasium和PettingZoo等多个环境下的实验表明，Gimitest能有效测试多种RL策略，显著提升测试覆盖面。该工具为强化学习策略的全面自动化测试提供了开源解决方案。
papers.abstract_en: Reinforcement learning (RL) policies can be unsafe and vulnerable to attacks. Ensuring their reliability is often a pain point as existing automated testing methods target only selected environments, testing scenarios, and RL algorithms. To address this, we propose a comprehensive framework for testing single- and multi-agent RL policies under varying conditions. Our implementation of this framework, Gimitest, is an open-source tool that supports various gym frameworks and allows for modifications of their integrated components. This article describes the framework and details Gimitest's functionality and architecture. It showcases its effectiveness in testing multiple RL policies in environments such as the official Farama Gymnasium and PettingZoo.

papers.title_en: Feedback Manipulation Regularization: Enabling Offline Agent Alignment for Imitation Learning
papers.evidence: 通过反馈将强化学习智能体与人类价值观对齐，可用于拟人化游戏bot
papers.tldr: 强化学习对齐日益重要，但现有方法多采用多阶段管道，局限于语言生成，难以在序列决策中进行单阶段联合训练。本文提出反馈操纵正则化（FMR），利用评估反馈作为纠正信号，提升模仿学习策略对齐性。在Safety Gymnasium上，FMR使多种模仿算法对齐性能提升，偏差减少高达98%，并在数据稀缺和噪声演示下保持鲁棒，为离线对齐提供高效方案。
papers.abstract_en: Reinforcement learning (RL) research has increasingly shifted focus towards alignment, ensuring agents learn behaviors adhering to human values. While human demonstrations and feedback have proven crucial for alignment, existing approaches predominantly combine these signals using multi-stage pipelines designed for the contextual bandit framing of language generation. Yet little work explores how these complementary inputs can serve as a richer, interconnected signal for single-stage offline training in fully sequential decision-making environments. We propose Feedback Manipulation Regularization (FMR), an algorithm-agnostic method that harnesses evaluative feedback as a corrective signal to improve the alignment of imitation learning policies. We adapt Safety Gymnasium environments to be a principled testbed for alignment evaluation, demonstrating improved aptitude and up to a 98\% reduction in misalignment across a range of imitation learning algorithms. FMR remains robust in limited data regimes, even when learning from scarce aligned and uninformative noisy demonstrations.
